title: Unguarded access to drift-prone JSON metadata property under StrictMode
description: Todo, bug, feature, and SIN records in this workspace exist in multiple schema generations. Legacy records may use fields like created, reported, affectedFiles, suggestedBy, or sourceId while newer records use created_at, file_refs, suggested_by, and source_id. Under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest, directly reading a missing property on a ConvertFrom-Json result throws 'The property X cannot be found on this object'. Code that processes workspace JSON must normalize aliases or explicitly guard property existence before access.
detection_method: Regex scan for direct access to drift-prone JSON metadata properties on common deserialized record variables without a nearby property-existence guard.
preventionRule: Never assume a ConvertFrom-Json record has a single canonical property set. Before reading drift-prone fields such as source_id, created_at, file_refs, created, or reported, either: 1) check PSObject.Properties.Name -contains '<field>', or 2) resolve through a normalization helper that tries canonical and legacy aliases in order.
remedy: Replace direct property access with guarded alias resolution. Example: $createdAt = Get-MyRecordPropertyValue -Record $item -Names @('created_at','createdAt','created','reported') -DefaultValue ''
